mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.25% represents a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses equity valuations through elevated discount rates. Sustained high rates above 5% compress forward earnings multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates as consumer purchasing power weakens, signaling demand contraction. A month-over-month decline in RSAFS below the prior 3-month trend predicts negative equity returns within 5 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns with directional accuracy > 52% in any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer income, which cascades into lower corporate earnings. When UNRATE rises above 4.5% and continues upward, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as recession risk materializes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 2 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, the mechanism indicates broad-based demand destruction that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side fragility that single-series signals miss.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1474K, RSAFS at 373946M as of June 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (Rank IC improvement < 0.01) versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and margin compression that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 52% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly capture labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while UNRATE rises, the mechanism signals labor market inflection that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138038K, UNRATE at 4.5% as of June 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term provides no incremental Rank IC improvement (< 0.01) over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 207.39 combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates the Federal Reserve is in active tightening mode. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as real rates remain restrictive and equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.39, FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of June 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents the fundamental demand trajectory. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized, it signals late-cycle weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 53% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.
